revision.text:
{{Object | Name=Nissan Skyline BNR34/R34 | Guid=3055-7591 | Deck=Nissan Skyline BNR34/R34 was an 10th generation model that was popular overseas. | Characters=Characters/Brian_OConnor,Characters/Tyler_Morgan | Concepts=Concepts/Nissan_Motor_Company | Franchises= | Games=Games/Asphalt_Urban_GT,Games/Drift_Spirits,Games/Forza_Motorsport,Games/Forza_Motorsport_2,Games/Forza_Motorsport_2023,Games/Forza_Motorsport_3,Games/Forza_Motorsport_4,Games/Forza_Motorsport_5,Games/Forza_Motorsport_6,Games/Gran_Turismo_2,Games/Gran_Turismo_2009,Games/Gran_Turismo_3_A_Spec,Games/Gran_Turismo_4,Games/Gran_Turismo_4_Prologue,Games/Gran_Turismo_5,Games/Gran_Turismo_5_Prologue,Games/Gran_Turismo_6,Games/Gran_Turismo_7,Games/Gran_Turismo_Sport,Games/Initial_D_Arcade_Stage_Zero,Games/Initial_D_Extreme_Stage,Games/Initial_D_Special_Stage,Games/Initial_D_Street_Stage,Games/Initial_D_The_Arcade,Games/Juiced,Games/Juiced_Eliminator,Games/Need_for_Speed,Games/Need_for_Speed_High_Stakes,Games/Need_for_Speed_No_Limits,Games/Need_for_Speed_Payback,Games/Need_for_Speed_Unbound,Games/Need_for_Speed_Underground,Games/The_Fast_and_the_Furious | Locations=Locations/Japan | People= | Objects=Objects/Car,Objects/Nissan_Skyline,Objects/Sports_Car | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/137/1377390/3645797-img_2626.jpeg }}

{{Object | Name=Nissan Skyline GT-R | Guid=3055-7434 | Deck=Nissan Skyline GT-R was a sports version of the Prince Skyline and the most popular model of Japan, with a drivetrain variants from FR to AWD. | Characters=Characters/Brian_OConnor | Concepts=Concepts/Licensed_Cars,Concepts/Nissan_Motor_Company | Franchises= | Games=Games/Asphalt_Urban_GT,Games/Asphalt_Urban_GT_2,Games/Chousoku_Henkei_Gyrozetter_Albatross_no_Tsubasa,Games/Drift_Spirits,Games/Driver_San_Francisco,Games/Fast_And_Furious_6_The_Game,Games/Fast_And_Furious_SuperCars,Games/Gran_Turismo,Games/Gran_Turismo_2,Games/Gran_Turismo_3_A_Spec,Games/Gran_Turismo_4,Games/Gran_Turismo_4_Prologue,Games/Gran_Turismo_5,Games/Gran_Turismo_5_Prologue,Games/Gran_Turismo_Concept_2001_Tokyo,Games/Gran_Turismo_Concept_2002_Tokyo_Geneva,Games/Gran_Turismo_Concept_2002_Tokyo_Seoul,Games/GRID_Autosport,Games/Juiced,Games/Juiced_2_Hot_Import_Nights,Games/Juiced_Eliminator,Games/Midnight_Club_3_DUB_Edition,Games/Midnight_Club_II,Games/Midnight_Club_Los_Angeles,Games/Need_for_Speed,Games/Need_for_Speed_Carbon,Games/Need_for_Speed_Heat,Games/Need_for_Speed_High_Stakes,Games/Need_for_Speed_Hot_Pursuit,Games/Need_for_Speed_No_Limits,Games/Need_for_Speed_Payback,Games/Need_for_Speed_ProStreet,Games/Need_for_Speed_Rivals,Games/Need_for_Speed_Shift,Games/Need_for_Speed_The_Run,Games/Need_for_Speed_Unbound,Games/Need_for_Speed_Undercover,Games/Need_for_Speed_Underground,Games/Need_for_Speed_Underground_2,Games/Nissan_presents_Over_Drivin_GT_R,Games/Project_CARS_3,Games/Real_Racing_3,Games/Road_And_Track_presents_The_Need_for_Speed,Games/Shift_2_Unleashed,Games/The_Fast_and_the_Furious,Games/The_Getaway,Games/Tokyo_Xtreme_Racer,Games/Tokyo_Xtreme_Racer_2,Games/Top_Drives | Locations=Locations/Japan | People= | Objects=Objects/Car,Objects/Sports_Car,Objects/Turbo | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/137/1377390/3600764-img_2101.png }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3600764-img_2101.png","path":"137\/1377390\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_avatar,square_mini,square_medium,square_small,scale_super,scale_small,scale_medium,scale_tiny,square_tiny,scale_avatar,screen_medium,scale_large,screen_kubrick","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> Nissan Skyline GT-R was a sports version of the Prince Skyline and the most popular model of Japan, with a drivetrain variants from FR to AWD.

{{Object | Name=Painting Knife | Guid=3055-7746 | Deck=A spatula for removing or applying paint to canvas. In games, it is often used not for artistic purposes, but to push something stuck or to move a locking latch | Characters= | Concepts=Concepts/Drawing,Concepts/Painter | Franchises= | Games=Games/Alone_in_the_Dark_2024 | Locations= | People= | Objects=Objects/Paint,Objects/Paint_Brush,Objects/Paintings }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":[],"background":[]}' /> A spatula for removing or applying paint to canvas. In games, it is often used not for artistic purposes, but to push something stuck or to move a locking latch

revision.comment: Point cover at a rendition that actually serves
revision.text:

<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":[],"background":[]}' />

{{Object | Name=Perilla | Guid=3055-7315 | Deck=Perilla is a leaf that is commonly used for Korean dishes. | Characters=Characters/Takayuki_Yagami | Concepts= | Franchises= | Games=Games/Lost_Judgment | Locations=Locations/Asia,Locations/Japan,Locations/Korea,Locations/Seoul,Locations/South_Korea,Locations/Tokyo | People= | Objects=Objects/Mint_Leaf | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/8/82962/3511493-perilla-leaf-blog-post-1.png }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3511493-perilla-leaf-blog-post-1.png","path":"8\/82962\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_medium,scale_medium,scale_super,square_small,scale_small,square_avatar,square_mini,square_tiny,scale_avatar,scale_large,screen_medium,screen_kubrick","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> Perilla is a leaf that is commonly used for Korean dishes.

revision.comment: Restore image alignment, sizes and captions from legacy source (3055-7504)
revision.text:
{{Object | Name=Peugeot 106 | Guid=3055-7504 | Deck=The 2004 Peugeot 106 GTi (Phase II) is a hot hatch manufactured by Peugeot from 1996 to 2003 for the European market. The GTi was the top of the line model of the 106 supermini lineup and available with a 1.6 litre DOHC engine producing 118 bhp. | Characters= | Concepts=Concepts/Downloadable_Content,Concepts/Licensed_Cars,Concepts/Peugeot | Franchises= | Games=Games/Enthusia_Professional_Racing,Games/Gran_Turismo_2009,Games/Gran_Turismo_4,Games/Gran_Turismo_5,Games/Gran_Turismo_6,Games/Need_for_Speed_Underground_2,Games/Need_for_Speed_V_Rally_2,Games/Top_Drives | Locations=Locations/France | People= | Objects=Objects/Peugeot_106_MAXI,Objects/Peugeot_206,Objects/Peugeot_306 | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/151/1511438/3645533-6745005551-360.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3645533-6745005551-360.jpg","path":"151\/1511438\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_medium,square_avatar,scale_super,square_small,square_tiny,scale_small,scale_medium,square_mini,scale_large,scale_avatar,screen_kubrick,screen_medium","caption":null},"background":[]}' />
===''NEED FOR SPEED: UNDERGROUND 2''=== The 106 appears in Asian and European releases of ''Need for Speed: Underground 2'' and can be selected as a starter car in the career mode.
Due to a low weight and short wheelbase, the 106 is a very nimble car that can take corners with little speed losses, although it is also prone to flipping over when curbing the road.
In terms of acceleration, the 106 is within the average of stage one cars but becomes significantly better with power upgrades.
{| class='wikitable gb-table-natural' |- | Unlock||Available from the beginning (PAL release) |- ! 0 - 60 mph||0 - 100 mph||Top Speed||Torque||Power |- | Stock||8.23 seconds||23.66 seconds||141.0 mph (227 km/h)||106.2 ft-lb||119.8 bhp |- | Fully Upgraded||3.26 seconds||7.87 seconds||207.0 mph (333 km/h)||277.5 ft-lb||394.7 bhp |}
==TRIVIA== * In the US PC release of ''Need for Speed: Underground 2'', the 106 GTi can be driven if the player applies an EU patch.

revision.comment: Restore image alignment, sizes and captions from legacy source (3055-7505)
revision.text:
{{Object | Name=Peugeot 206 | Guid=3055-7505 | Deck=The Peugeot 206 is a city compact car created and manufactured by French car company Peugeot. The 206 is the best-selling Peugeot model of all time with 8,358,217 cars sold by 2012. | Characters= | Concepts=Concepts/Licensed_Cars,Concepts/Peugeot | Franchises= | Games=Games/EA_Sports_WRC,Games/Forza_Horizon,Games/Forza_Motorsport,Games/Forza_Motorsport_2,Games/Forza_Motorsport_3,Games/Forza_Motorsport_4,Games/Gran_Turismo_2009,Games/Gran_Turismo_3_A_Spec,Games/Gran_Turismo_4,Games/Gran_Turismo_5,Games/Gran_Turismo_6,Games/Metropolis_Street_Racer,Games/Need_for_Speed_Underground,Games/Need_for_Speed_Underground_2,Games/Need_for_Speed_V_Rally_2,Games/Sega_GT,Games/Sega_GT_2002,Games/The_Getaway,Games/Top_Drives,Games/V_Rally_3 | Locations=Locations/France,Locations/Mulhouse,Locations/Poissy | People= | Objects=Objects/Car,Objects/Peugeot_106,Objects/Peugeot_206_WRC,Objects/Peugeot_208_T16,Objects/Peugeot_306 | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/151/1511438/3644826-3677256882-1200.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3644826-3677256882-1200.jpg","path":"151\/1511438\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_medium,scale_super,square_medium,square_avatar,square_small,square_mini,scale_small,square_tiny,scale_avatar,scale_large,screen_kubrick,screen_medium","caption":null},"background":[]}' />
===''NEED FOR SPEED: UNDERGROUND''=== The 2003 206 appears in ''Need for Speed: Underground''.
It boasts quick acceleration and good handling, making it up to par with other starter cars. When fully upgraded, the 206 also becomes capable of reaching a high top speed.
====STOCK====
===''NEED FOR SPEED: UNDERGROUND 2''=== The 206 appears in ''Need for Speed: Underground 2'' from the beginning and can be selected as a starter car in the career mode.
{| class='wikitable gb-table-natural' |- | Unlock||Available from the beginning |- ! 0 - 60 mph||0 - 100 mph||Top Speed||Torque||Power |- | Stock||8.32 seconds||21.00 seconds||125.0 mph (201 km/h)||141.5 ft-lb||138.0 bhp |- | Fully Upgraded||3.12 seconds||8.54 seconds||207.0 mph (333 km/h)||278.0 ft-lb||398.8 bhp |}

{{Object | Name=Peugeot 206 WRC | Guid=3055-7565 | Characters= | Concepts= | Franchises= | Games=Games/WRC_II_Extreme,Games/WRC_Rally_Evolved | Locations= | People= | Objects=Objects/Peugeot_106_MAXI,Objects/Peugeot_206,Objects/Peugeot_306_MAXI }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":[],"background":[]}' />

{{Object | Name=Peugeot 208 T16 | Guid=3055-7554 | Deck=Based on the design of the road-going 208, the Peugeot 208 T16 Pikes Peak is built for competing at the Pikes Peak International Hill Climb. | Characters=Characters/Sebastien_Loeb | Concepts=Concepts/Licensed_Cars,Concepts/Rally | Franchises= | Games=Games/DiRT_4,Games/DiRT_Rally,Games/Dirt_Rally_2_0,Games/EA_Sports_WRC,Games/Sebastien_Loeb_Rally_Evo,Games/Top_Drives,Games/WRC_5,Games/WRC_6,Games/WRC_7 | Locations=Locations/France | People= | Objects=Objects/Peugeot_205_T16,Objects/Peugeot_206,Objects/Peugeot_207_Cup_Super_2000,Objects/Peugeot_405_T16_Pikes_Peak | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/151/1511438/3645149-4324021809-344.j.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3645149-4324021809-344.j.jpg","path":"151\/1511438\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_small,square_avatar,square_medium,scale_medium,scale_small,square_mini,square_tiny,scale_super,scale_avatar,scale_large,screen_kubrick,screen_medium","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> Based on the design of the road-going 208, the '''Peugeot 208 T16 Pikes Peak''' is built for competing at the Pikes Peak International Hill Climb. It is fitted with the rear wing found from the Peugeot 908, and a twin-turbocharged 3.2-litre engine which produces around 875 bhp. Driven by nine-times WRC champion Sébastien Loeb, the 208 T16 broke Rhys Millen's previous record time of 9:46.16, set in 2012 on the first fully paved roads in the history of the competition, with the a new record time of 8:13.878 on 30 June 2013.

{{Object | Name=Peugeot 306 | Guid=3055-7506 | Deck=The Peugeot 306 is a small family car built by the French car manufacturer Peugeot from 1993 to 2002. It replaced the 309. | Characters= | Concepts=Concepts/Licensed_Cars,Concepts/Peugeot,Concepts/The_1990s | Franchises= | Games=Games/Gran_Turismo_2,Games/Metropolis_Street_Racer,Games/Need_for_Speed_V_Rally_2,Games/Sega_GT,Games/The_Getaway,Games/TOCA_Touring_Car_Championship,Games/Top_Drives | Locations=Locations/France,Locations/Poissy | People= | Objects=Objects/Peugeot_106,Objects/Peugeot_206,Objects/Peugeot_306_MAXI | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/151/1511438/3645564-8277444437-850.j.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3645564-8277444437-850.j.jpg","path":"151\/1511438\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_mini,square_avatar,square_medium,scale_medium,scale_super,scale_small,square_small,square_tiny,scale_avatar,scale_large,screen_kubrick,screen_medium","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> It took Peugeot a number of years to come up with a true successor to the legendary 205 GTI, but when the 306 GTI-6 finally arrived in 1996 it didn't disappoint. Powered by a two-litre 16 valve engine that produces 167BHP at 6,500rpm, the 306 is the most powerful hot hatch of its type and the most powerful production car ever built at Peugeot's UK production plant. The engine produces enough grunt to push the 306 from 0-60mph in 8.2 seconds and on to a top speed of 137mph. In a first for a car in its class, the 306 also boasts a six-speed manual gearbox. Originally designed by Peugeot for competition purposes, engineers decided that its closer ratios (compared with the standard five-speed box) would further improve the sports credentials of the GTI-6. Fortunately, Peugeot's renowned chassis engineering ensures the 306 handles as well as it goes. On the front, it utilises pseudo-MacPherson type struts with wishbones and anti-roll bar, while at the rear, Peugeot's innovative self-steering rear axle is incorporated to stabilise the car in bends. Brakes have been uprated according to the car's performance with 28cm discs on the front and rear and a four-sensor ABS system. The GTI also gets 6Jx15 rims made of light alloy and 195/55R15 tyres, although there is little else to distinguish it from the rest of the range on the outside. Despite the fact that it has now been around for several years, according to the press, the 306 is still the best handling hot hatch around. The GTI-6 is also joined by the Rallye version which boasts the same running gear, but a stripped down interior to reduce weight by 16kg and thereby improve performance.

{{Object | Name=Peugeot 9X8 | Guid=3055-7690 | Characters= | Concepts= | Franchises= | Games=Games/Le_Mans_Ultimate | Locations= | People= | Objects= }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":[],"background":[]}' />

{{Object | Name=Pigtails | Guid=3055-7680 | Deck=Hairstyle with two ponytails, possibly also braided into braids | Characters=Characters/Natalie_16123,Characters/Nina_27334 | Concepts=Concepts/Side_Ponytail | Franchises= | Games=Games/Ape_Escape,Games/Ape_Escape_2,Games/Ape_Escape_Pumped_And_Primed,Games/Omori,Games/Rogue_Galaxy,Games/Threads_of_Fate,Games/Yaiba_Ninja_Gaiden_Z | Locations= | People= | Objects=Objects/Dreadlocks,Objects/Hairband,Objects/Mullet,Objects/Pompadour,Objects/Ponytail | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/173/1734046/3714550-roguegalaxy-mio.jpg }}

{{Object | Name=Ping Pong Table | Guid=3055-7663 | Characters= | Concepts=Concepts/Table_Tennis | Franchises= | Games=Games/Contract_J_A_C_K,Games/Ping_Pong,Games/Ping_Pong_2013,Games/Red_Faction,Games/Rockstar_Games_presents_Table_Tennis,Games/VR_Ping_Pong,Games/Wii_Sports_Resort,Games/Wii_Sports_Wii_Sports_Resort | Locations= | People= | Objects=Objects/Ping_Pong_Ball,Objects/Table_tennis_racket | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/0/26/1296516-tt_00_o2.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"1296516-tt_00_o2.jpg","path":"0\/26\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_tiny,square_medium,scale_medium,square_avatar,screen_medium,scale_large,scale_small,square_mini,square_small,scale_avatar,ignore_jpg_scale_medium,scale_super,scale_kubrick,screen_kubrick,scale_tiny","caption":""},"background":[]}' />

{{Object | Name=Polaroid Photos | Guid=3055-7732 | Deck=Instantly taken photos on Polaroid cameras. Their trademark white frame has become so recognizable in popular culture that they are used as the very concept of photography, without even mentioning the name of the manufacturing company | Characters= | Concepts=Concepts/Photography | Franchises= | Games=Games/Condemned_Criminal_Origins,Games/Hitman_3,Games/inFamous_2,Games/Life_Is_Strange,Games/Omori,Games/The_Suffering,Games/The_Suffering_Ties_That_Bind | Locations= | People= | Objects=Objects/Camera,Objects/Photo | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/173/1734046/3782594-omori-polaroidphotos.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3782594-omori-polaroidphotos.jpg","path":"173\/1734046\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_small,scale_medium,square_mini,scale_super,square_medium,scale_small,square_tiny,square_avatar,scale_tiny","caption":"Omori - Polaroid Photos"},"background":[]}' /> They can often be seen in games with a fun atmosphere. As well as in horror, detective adventure games and where the characters have psychological problems, the developer can use them to contact the player, showing various reminders of the character's dark past, and leaving traces of blood, fingerprints or strange inscriptions on their harmless white frame.

{{Object | Name=Pontiac Aztek | Guid=3055-7448 | Deck=Pontiac Aztek is Pontiac’s first SUV model that was produced in 2001. | Characters=Characters/Walter_White | Concepts=Concepts/Licensed_Cars,Concepts/Pontiac | Franchises= | Games=Games/Forza_Motorsport_6,Games/Top_Drives | Locations=Locations/The_United_States_of_America | People= | Objects=Objects/Car,Objects/Sports_Utility_Vehicle | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/137/1377390/3608255-img_2405.jpeg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3608255-img_2405.jpeg","path":"137\/1377390\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_medium,square_small,scale_small,scale_medium,scale_super,square_avatar,square_mini,square_tiny,scale_tiny,scale_avatar,screen_kubrick,scale_large,screen_medium","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> Pontiac Aztek is Pontiac’s first SUV model that was produced in 2001.

revision.comment: Restore content truncated by the legacy migration (3055-7437, guid-audit)
revision.text:
{{Object | Name=Pontiac Firebird | Guid=3055-7437 | Deck=Pontiac Firebird/Trans Am is a front and rear engine muscle car that shares the same platform with the Chevrolet Camaro. | Characters=Characters/K_I_T_T,Characters/Michael_Knight,Characters/Snake_27203 | Concepts=Concepts/Licensed_Cars,Concepts/Pontiac | Franchises= | Games=Games/Enthusia_Professional_Racing,Games/Forza_Horizon,Games/Forza_Horizon_2,Games/Forza_Horizon_3,Games/Forza_Horizon_4,Games/Forza_Horizon_5,Games/Forza_Motorsport_2023,Games/Forza_Motorsport_3,Games/Forza_Motorsport_4,Games/Forza_Motorsport_5,Games/Forza_Motorsport_6,Games/Global_Touring_Challenge_Africa,Games/Gran_Turismo_6,Games/Gran_Turismo_7,Games/Juiced,Games/Juiced_2_Hot_Import_Nights,Games/Juiced_Eliminator,Games/Midnight_Club_Los_Angeles,Games/Midnight_Club_Los_Angeles_Remix,Games/Need_for_Speed_Carbon_Own_the_City,Games/Need_for_Speed_Heat,Games/Need_for_Speed_High_Stakes,Games/Need_for_Speed_Payback,Games/Need_for_Speed_Unbound,Games/Need_for_Speed_Undercover,Games/Project_Gotham_Racing_4,Games/Street_Rod_2_The_Next_Generation,Games/The_Crew_2,Games/The_Crew_Motorfest | Locations=Locations/Canada,Locations/The_United_States_of_America | People= | Objects=Objects/Car,Objects/Muscle_Car | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/137/1377390/3631019-img_2570.jpeg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3631019-img_2570.jpeg","path":"137\/1377390\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_avatar,scale_medium,scale_small,square_small,square_medium,square_tiny,square_mini,scale_super,scale_tiny,scale_avatar,scale_large,screen_kubrick,screen_medium","caption":null},"background":[]}' />
The Pontiac Firebird/Trans Am was a front engined rear wheel drive North American Muscle car that was produced by Pontiac and a counterpart cousin to GM’s model car brand, Chevrolet Camaro.
==History== In 1966, after the Firebird 1 was made and experimented in the test track in 1954. Pontiac produced the F-body, equivalent to the Chevrolet Camaro called, the Firebird (that named after the GM’s prototype test car, the Firebird 1). The first generation Firebird that shared the same models of the GTO and Grand Prix. The Firebird’s engine was 230 cu in (3.8 L) SOHC inline-six, fitted with a one-barrel Rochester carburator producing 165 horsepower (123 kW).

{{Object | Name=Pontiac GTO | Guid=3055-7438 | Deck=The Pontiac GTO was a rebadged model of the Tempest that was produced in 1963. | Characters= | Concepts=Concepts/Pontiac | Franchises= | Games=Games/Forza_Horizon_3,Games/Forza_Horizon_4,Games/Forza_Horizon_5,Games/Forza_Motorsport,Games/Forza_Motorsport_2,Games/Forza_Motorsport_3,Games/Forza_Motorsport_4,Games/Forza_Motorsport_7,Games/Juiced_2_Hot_Import_Nights,Games/Midnight_Club_Los_Angeles,Games/Midnight_Club_Los_Angeles_Remix,Games/Need_for_Speed_Most_Wanted,Games/Need_for_Speed_Underground_2,Games/Street_Rod_2_The_Next_Generation,Games/Top_Drives | Locations=Locations/The_United_States_of_America | People= | Objects=Objects/Car,Objects/Muscle_Car | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/137/1377390/3608260-img_2408.jpeg }}

{{Object | Name=Potato Wedges | Guid=3055-7308 | Deck=Potato Wedges are baked or fried forms of potatoes that are common as sides with dishes like fried chicken. | Characters=Characters/Takayuki_Yagami | Concepts= | Franchises= | Games=Games/Lost_Judgment | Locations= | People= | Objects=Objects/French_Fries,Objects/Fried_Chicken,Objects/Mac_And_Cheese,Objects/Macaroni,Objects/Potato,Objects/Potato_Chips | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/8/82962/3511479-970091333.png }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3511479-970091333.png","path":"8\/82962\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"scale_super,square_mini,square_medium,square_small,scale_small,scale_medium,square_avatar,square_tiny,scale_avatar,scale_large,screen_medium,screen_kubrick","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> Potato Wedges are baked or fried forms of potatoes that are common as sides with dishes like fried chicken.

{{Object | Name=Power Shovel | Guid=3055-7465 | Aliases=Excavator,Digger,Scooper,Mechanical Shovel,Steam Shovel | Characters= | Concepts=Concepts/Build_Queue,Concepts/Construction,Concepts/Mining,Concepts/Roadworks | Franchises= | Games=Games/Anno_2070,Games/BCV_Battle_Construction_Vehicles,Games/Construction_Destruction,Games/Emergency_5,Games/Excavator_Simulator,Games/Injustice_2,Games/LEGO_Dimensions,Games/LEGO_Harry_Potter_Years_5_7,Games/Mega_Man_Star_Force,Games/Power_Shovel,Games/PowerWash_Simulator,Games/Sonic_Racing_CrossWorlds | Locations=Locations/Construction_Site,Locations/Excavation_Site | People= | Objects=Objects/Bulldozer,Objects/Concrete_Mixer,Objects/Dump_Truck,Objects/Steam_Roller,Objects/Tow_Truck,Objects/Truck,Objects/Wrecking_ball | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/16/164924/3628011-5951521010-power.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3628011-5951521010-power.jpg","path":"16\/164924\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_small,square_small,square_medium,scale_tiny,square_avatar,scale_super,scale_medium,square_mini,square_tiny,scale_large,scale_avatar,screen_medium,screen_kubrick","caption":null},"background":[]}' />
